title: Introduce granular migration target format and correct migrations.json
description:
Replace the coarse 'workflows' / 'knowledge-base' regeneration targets with a colon-delimited sub-target format (e.g. 'workflows:plan_task', 'knowledge-base:architecture'). This lets migrations declare exactly which files changed, so multi-version upgrades regenerate the minimal set rather than everything.

Format rules:
- Granular: 'workflows:plan_task' regenerates only .forge/workflows/plan_task.md
- Bare (legacy): 'workflows' short-circuits to full category rebuild (dominance rule)
- Same applies to 'knowledge-base:architecture', 'knowledge-base:business-domain', 'knowledge-base:stack-checklist'
- 'commands' stays atomic (already fine-grained enough)
- Aggregation across multi-hop migrations: union of all sub-targets; if any hop has a bare category, that category dominates

Changes required:
1. forge/commands/update.md — update Step 2A/2B aggregation logic and Step 4 regeneration dispatch to split targets on ':' and invoke regenerate.md with the specific sub-target. Add the dominance rule. The '/forge:regenerate <category> <sub-target>' invocation syntax must be wired up.
2. forge/commands/regenerate.md — update the arguments section to document the colon format. Ensure each category handler accepts an optional named sub-target and regenerates only that file when present.
3. forge/migrations.json — three corrections:
   a. Strip 'tools' from every past migration entry that lists it
   b. Correct the 0.5.9→0.6.0 entry: change 'regenerate' from ['tools', 'workflows'] to ['workflows:sprint_intake', 'workflows:sprint_plan'] — these are the only two workflows that changed in S02
   c. Add the 0.6.0→0.6.1 entry (lean migration architecture — no user-facing regeneration needed)

Note on backwards compat: old migration entries with bare 'workflows' or 'knowledge-base' continue to trigger full rebuilds. No existing upgrade paths break.
